title: ds-viz-linkgraph - option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.linkgraph. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: backgroundColor, fieldOrder, linkColor, linkWidth, nodeColor, nodeTextColor, nodeHeight, nodeHighlightColor, nodeTextHighlightColor, nodeSpacingX, nodeSpacingY, nodeWidth, resultLimit, showNodeCounts, showValueCounts.

ds_meals.name: canonical PDF example - meals Breakfast Lunch Dinner

ds_kill_chain.name: SOC kill chain - actor x technique x asset x action

ds_org.name: org chart - region x team x lead

options.query: | makeresults count=10 | streamstats count AS rn | eval region = case(rn<=4,"NA", rn<=7,"EMEA", 1==1,"APAC") | eval team = case(rn==1,"Platform", rn==2,"Apps", rn==3,"Data", rn==4,"SRE", rn==5,"Apps", rn==6,"Data", rn==7,"Security", rn==8,"Apps", rn==9,"Data", rn==10,"Mobile") | eval lead = case(rn==1,"Alex", rn==2,"Sam", rn==3,"Jordan", rn==4,"Taylor", rn==5,"Morgan", rn==6,"Jamie", rn==7,"Casey", rn==8,"Riley", rn==9,"Quinn", rn==10,"Avery") | table region team lead

ds_wide.name: wide data set to test resultLimit 50 distinct nodes

options.query: | makeresults count=80 | streamstats count AS rn | eval colA = "A_".tostring(rn%30) | eval colB = "B_".tostring(rn%25) | eval colC = "C_".tostring(rn%18) | table colA colB colC

ds_kill_chain_full.name: 5-column MITRE-style attack chain - 60+ events for a fully populated graph

options.query: | makeresults count=60 | streamstats count AS rn | eval Tactic = case(rn%5==0,"Reconnaissance", rn%5==1,"Initial Access", rn%5==2,"Execution", rn%5==3,"Persistence", 1==1,"Impact") | eval Technique = case(Tactic=="Reconnaissance" AND rn%2==0,"Active Scanning", Tactic=="Reconnaissance","Phishing for Info", Tactic=="Initial Access" AND rn%3==0,"Spear-phishing", Tactic=="Initial Access" AND rn%3==1,"Public-Facing App", Tactic=="Initial Access","Valid Accounts", Tactic=="Execution" AND rn%2==0,"PowerShell", Tactic=="Execution","Native API", Tactic=="Persistence" AND rn%2==0,"Scheduled Task", Tactic=="Persistence","Registry Run Keys", rn%3==0,"Data Encrypted", rn%3==1,"Data Destruction", 1==1,"Account Access Removal") | eval Asset = case(rn%6==0,"web-edge-01", rn%6==1,"db-prod-02", rn%6==2,"ad-srv-01", rn%6==3,"file-srv-01", rn%6==4,"workstation-laptop-19", 1==1,"jump-host-01") | eval Severity = case(rn%4==0,"Critical", rn%4==1,"High", rn%4==2,"Medium", 1==1,"Low") | eval Action = case(rn%5==0,"Block", rn%5==1,"Quarantine", rn%5==2,"Alert", rn%5==3,"Investigate", 1==1,"Allow") | table Tactic Technique Asset Severity Action

ds_itsm_flow.name: ITSM workflow - 80 tickets across team x category x priority x state x assignee

options.query: | makeresults count=80 | streamstats count AS rn | eval Team = case(rn%5==0,"Platform", rn%5==1,"AppDev", rn%5==2,"Network", rn%5==3,"Security", 1==1,"DataOps") | eval Category = case(rn%6==0,"Outage", rn%6==1,"Performance", rn%6==2,"Access", rn%6==3,"Bug", rn%6==4,"Feature", 1==1,"Question") | eval Priority = case(rn%4==0,"P1", rn%4==1,"P2", rn%4==2,"P3", 1==1,"P4") | eval State = case(rn%4==0,"Open", rn%4==1,"In Progress", rn%4==2,"Pending", 1==1,"Resolved") | eval Assignee = case(rn%8==0,"alice", rn%8==1,"bob", rn%8==2,"carol", rn%8==3,"dave", rn%8==4,"eve", rn%8==5,"frank", rn%8==6,"grace", 1==1,"henry") | table Team Category Priority State Assignee

ds_dependency_chain.name: service dependency chain - User -> App -> Service -> Database -> Storage (5 columns, 100 rows)

options.query: | makeresults count=100 | streamstats count AS rn | eval User = case(rn%4==0,"customer", rn%4==1,"admin", rn%4==2,"analyst", 1==1,"system") | eval App = case(rn%6==0,"web-portal", rn%6==1,"mobile-ios", rn%6==2,"mobile-android", rn%6==3,"api-gateway", rn%6==4,"batch-job", 1==1,"cli-tool") | eval Service = case(rn%5==0,"auth-svc", rn%5==1,"billing-svc", rn%5==2,"search-svc", rn%5==3,"recommend-svc", 1==1,"notify-svc") | eval Database = case(rn%4==0,"users-db", rn%4==1,"orders-db", rn%4==2,"events-db", 1==1,"cache-redis") | eval Storage = case(rn%3==0,"s3-primary", rn%3==1,"s3-backup", 1==1,"glacier-archive") | table User App Service Database Storage

ds_network_flow.name: network 4-tuple - SrcSubnet x Protocol x DstPort x DstService (80 flows)

options.query: | makeresults count=80 | streamstats count AS rn | eval SrcSubnet = case(rn%5==0,"10.0.1.0/24", rn%5==1,"10.0.2.0/24", rn%5==2,"172.16.0.0/16", rn%5==3,"192.168.10.0/24", 1==1,"203.0.113.0/24") | eval Protocol = case(rn%3==0,"TCP", rn%3==1,"UDP", 1==1,"ICMP") | eval DstPort = case(rn%6==0,"443", rn%6==1,"80", rn%6==2,"22", rn%6==3,"3389", rn%6==4,"5432", 1==1,"8443") | eval DstService = case(DstPort=="443","https-frontend", DstPort=="80","http-frontend", DstPort=="22","ssh-bastion", DstPort=="3389","rdp-jumpbox", DstPort=="5432","postgres-prod", 1==1,"https-internal") | table SrcSubnet Protocol DstPort DstService

ds_attack_path_compact.name: compact attack path - actor x stage x outcome (3 cols, 40 rows for vivid clusters)

options.query: | makeresults count=40 | streamstats count AS rn | eval Actor = case(rn%4==0,"APT-Bear", rn%4==1,"FIN-Crime", rn%4==2,"Insider", 1==1,"Botnet") | eval Stage = case(rn%5==0,"Phishing", rn%5==1,"Lateral Movement", rn%5==2,"Privilege Escalation", rn%5==3,"Data Exfiltration", 1==1,"Encryption") | eval Outcome = case(rn%3==0,"Blocked", rn%3==1,"Detected", 1==1,"Successful") | table Actor Stage Outcome

options.markdown:
# `splunk.linkgraph` - option matrix

Link graphs visualize **discrete relationships across multiple categorical columns**. Each column becomes a stack of nodes; links connect adjacent columns where pairs co-occur.

Full options list from the **10.4 PDF**:
- `backgroundColor`
- `fieldOrder` (DOS - controls column order)
- `linkColor`, `linkWidth`
- `nodeColor`, `nodeTextColor`
- `nodeHighlightColor`, `nodeTextHighlightColor`
- `nodeWidth`, `nodeHeight`, `nodeSpacingX`, `nodeSpacingY`
- `resultLimit` (default `50` per column)
- `showNodeCounts`, `showValueCounts`

**Note**: Links only appear between adjacent columns - reorder columns via `fieldOrder` to control which relationships render.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - canonical PDF example
viz_default.description: Breakfast x Lunch x Dinner. Default node and link colors, default sizing.

viz_pdf_styled.title: 2. PDF-styled (yellow nodes, red links)
viz_pdf_styled.description: Reproduces the PDF example styling exactly.

viz_kill_chain.title: 3. SOC kill chain - 4 columns
viz_kill_chain.description: Actor x Technique x Asset x Action. Demonstrates 4-column rendering.

viz_field_order.title: 4. fieldOrder reorders columns
viz_field_order.description: fieldOrder explicitly puts Action first, then Technique, then Actor.

viz_field_order_subset.title: 5. fieldOrder excludes a column
viz_field_order_subset.description: Unspecified fields are dropped entirely. Only Actor and Asset render here.

viz_highlight_colors.title: 6. nodeHighlightColor + nodeTextHighlightColor
viz_highlight_colors.description: Click any node to see custom highlight colors (red on dark text).

viz_thick_links.title: 7. linkWidth=8 - thick links
viz_thick_links.description: Use thick links when relationships are the headline. Becomes noisy past ~20 connections.

viz_compact.title: 8. Compact - small nodes, tight spacing
viz_compact.description: nodeWidth=120, nodeHeight=18, nodeSpacingY=4. Fits more rows per panel.

viz_spacious.title: 9. Spacious - large nodes, wide gaps
viz_spacious.description: Hero panel sizing - few items, lots of breathing room.

viz_counts_off.title: 10. showNodeCounts/showValueCounts=false
viz_counts_off.description: Counts hidden - cleaner but loses the magnitude story.

viz_counts_on.title: 11. showNodeCounts/showValueCounts=true (defaults)
viz_counts_on.description: Counts visible - column header shows total nodes; node label shows occurrence count.

viz_result_limit_low.title: 12. resultLimit=8 - truncated columns
viz_result_limit_low.description: Only first 8 nodes per column render. Wider data shows truncation badge.

viz_tinted_background.title: 13. Custom backgroundColor
viz_tinted_background.description: Editorial panel tint with brand-coordinated nodes.

options.markdown:
## Bigger graphs - more nodes, more links

The panels below stress-test `splunk.linkgraph` with **5 columns** and **40-100 rows** so you can see how the layout behaves with a fully populated graph. Set `resultLimit` per column to control density - the default is `50`. When the graph gets dense, increase `nodeSpacingY` and `linkWidth` to keep the relationships readable.

viz_kill_chain_5col.title: 14. MITRE-style 5-column attack chain
viz_kill_chain_5col.description: Tactic -> Technique -> Asset -> Severity -> Action across 60 events. This is the canonical SOC layout: each column tells a different question (what happened, how, where, how bad, what we did). Larger nodes (220 wide) and dark slate panel mimic a SOC wall.

viz_itsm_5col.title: 15. ITSM ticket flow - 5 columns, 80 rows
viz_itsm_5col.description: Team -> Category -> Priority -> State -> Assignee. Reads as a service-management story: the bigger a node, the more tickets share that value. Widen yAxisLabelWidth equivalent (nodeWidth) so long assignee names render. Dense - good demo of how `nodeSpacingY: 8` and `linkWidth: 1` keep it scannable.

viz_service_chain_5col.title: 16. Service dependency chain - User to Storage
viz_service_chain_5col.description: User -> App -> Service -> Database -> Storage. Demonstrates a five-stage service map with 100 rows of dependency data. Brand-coordinated palette - violet nodes, teal links - and showValueCounts on so you can see traffic concentration.

viz_network_4col.title: 17. Network 4-tuple flow - subnet to service
viz_network_4col.description: SrcSubnet -> Protocol -> DstPort -> DstService. Full-width 4-column layout, 80 flows. The Protocol column is intentionally narrow (3 distinct values: TCP/UDP/ICMP) so it acts as a visual gate between source and destination - any traffic that goes through ICMP is immediately visible. Promoted to full row so the SrcSubnet labels (long CIDR strings) get the room they need.

viz_kill_chain_5col_inverted.title: 18. Same data, fieldOrder inverted - read right-to-left
viz_kill_chain_5col_inverted.description: Same 60-event SOC data as panel 14 but with fieldOrder reversed (Action -> Severity -> Asset -> Technique -> Tactic). Useful when the story starts from the response ("what did we do?") and traces back to the cause. Same graph, different narrative direction.

viz_attack_path_thick.title: 19. Hero attack path - thick links across the full row
viz_attack_path_thick.description: Actor -> Stage -> Outcome with `linkWidth: 8` so the dominant pathways read as a graphic, not a chart. 40 rows means a few combinations dominate, which is exactly when thick links work. Promoted to full-width hero panel - 320px-wide nodes with generous nodeSpacingX so the typography is the headline.

viz_dense_test.title: 20. Density test - resultLimit 50 (default)
viz_dense_test.description: Largest possible single panel: ITSM data at the default resultLimit of 50 nodes per column. Panel intentionally tall (h=560) so the chart can breathe. Use this layout when the story is *every* relationship matters - typically forensic and audit views, not at-a-glance dashboards.
